LAW AND MOTION, 301, PLAINTIFF MAHER SOLIMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MAHER SOLIMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CARRY HIS BURDEN AS TO HIS COMPLAINT. IN ANY EVENT, DEFENDANT HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO RESPONSIBILITY FOR ALLOWING UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS TO THE ACCOUNTS AND NOTICE OF PROBLEMS WHICH AFFECTS EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY DENIED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT'S CROSS-COMPLAINT RENEWED MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

OBJECTION TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY X-COMP IN OPPOS. TO MTN FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT SOLIMAN, MAHER W

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PLAINTIFF MAHER SOLIMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, HEARSAY OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS A AND B TO THE MACDOUGAL DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. THEREFORE IT IS UNDISPUTED FOR PURPOSES OF THIS MOTION THAT PLAINTIFF HAD NO OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN THE JOINT ACCOUNT. NONE THE LESS, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 AND 3 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HIS SEPARATE ACCOUNT DID NOT RECEIVE MONEY TO WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ENTITLED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED THAT HE OWED NO DUTY TO DEFENDANT. JOINT ORDER TO BE PREPARED BY PARTIES PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2007 Continued To Dec-17-07 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
MAHER W. SOLIMAN, IN PROPRIA PERSONA, (415)776-3998, PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY
KEVIN MACDOUGALD, ESQ., (415)492-8734, DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MAHER SOLIMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO CARRY HIS BURDEN AS TO HIS COMPLAINT. IN ANY EVENT, DEFENDANT HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO RESPONSIBILITY FOR ALLOWING UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS TO THE ACCOUNTS AND NOTICE OF PROBLEMS WHICH AFFECTS EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY DENIED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANT'S CROSS-COMPLAINT RENEWED MOTION. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (301/PJB/LB)
